page: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 96-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, the CPU features 1.0 thread per core and is supported by 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read bandwidth for small cached blocks and strong performance for larger uncached blocks. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong performance in static web serving. For large language model inference, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for both 7B and 70B models, though prompt processing speeds fall into the strong category.

The primary hardware tradeoff of the c7a.24xlarge is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of physical cores and DDR5 memory bandwidth provides high compute throughput per resource unit, making it highly efficient for compute-bound workloads. This instance is suited for CPU-intensive tasks such as software compilation, batch processing, ray tracing, high-performance web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 38 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis operations, with strong memory bandwidth and static web serving capabilities. However, the low memory-to-core ratio and lack of local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs. The c7a.24xlarge is highly efficient for compute-dense workloads, making it suitable for software development pipelines, ray tracing, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a 96-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance with 192 GB DDR5 RAM and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, 96-core compute instance featuring DDR5 memory and top-tier multi-threaded performance for intensive CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz.
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core).
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis performance.
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Suited for batch processing, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
